title: USDZ Package Layout Productions
summary: ZIP/USDZ structure rules required before a package entry can be handed to a layer-format parser.

productions.name: zip_eocd_and_central_directory
requires: find the end-of-central-directory record within the ZIP comment search window
read the non-Zip64 central directory range
reject multi-disk archives and out-of-bounds central directory ranges
productions.errata_note: AOUSD §16.4.1.4 requires the EOCD comment length to be zero. §16.4.2 permits readers to accept out-of-spec files such as a package carrying a comment. This reader accepts any comment length that is consistent with the file size (eocd_offset + 22 + comment_length == file_size); it still requires the EOCD to run to end-of-file with no trailing padding. Reader-only leniency; USDZ writing is out of scope.

productions.name: central_entry_to_local_data
requires: validate the corresponding local file header
compute file data offset as local header offset plus fixed header, name, and local extra lengths
validate compressed-size bounds against the opened package bytes

productions.name: usdz_entry_constraints
requires: stored compressed and uncompressed sizes match
productions.errata_note: AOUSD §16.4.1.3 prose says 'every file header starts at a multiple of 64 bytes'. The reference USD writer (pxr UsdZipFileWriter) instead aligns the entry DATA to 64 bytes via local-header extra-field padding; the header offset may be unaligned. The data-aligned reading is normative for this repository: the alignment check applies to data_offset = local_header_offset + 30 + local_name_len + local_extra_len, computed from the local header. A reader must not require the local-header offset itself to be 64-aligned.

productions.name: entry_ordering
requires: entries exposed by UsdzArchiveIndex are ordered by physical local-header offset
default-layer selection returns the first ordered entry without scanning for a later USD extension
productions.errata_note: AOUSD §16.4.1.2 requires the root layer to be both the first central-directory entry and the first file physically; a conformant writer makes these coincide. This reader selects the default layer by physical order (lowest local-header offset) and does not separately verify central-directory order, nor scan past the first entry for a USD extension. Reader-only leniency; §16.4.2 permits it.

laws: local file payload bytes are never copied into a new package-entry buffer when a byte view into the opened package is available
directory traversal or platform-specific path reinterpretation must not happen during entry lookup
